info.title: CAT Platform API
info.description: Computerized Adaptive Testing platform with hybrid neural-IRT estimation

post.description:
Public self-registration. Always creates an examinee account; clients
cannot choose role. Administrator/researcher accounts are minted via the
privileged POST /admin/users endpoint (administrator-only).

OAuth2 client-credentials grant for machine-to-machine callers
(RFC 6749 §4.4). Client authentication via form fields or HTTP Basic.

Mints a short-lived service JWT carrying the least-privilege scope set
(SERVICE_SCOPES). Disabled entirely unless SERVICE_CLIENT_SECRET is
configured. Deactivating the service principal row (DELETE /admin/users)
is the operator kill switch — the grant then returns invalid_client.

schema.title: Response List Item Banks Item Banks  Get
Response List Items Item Banks  Item Bank Id  Items Get

post.description:
Sync ingest — kept for small banks, dry runs, and scripted callers.

For anything larger than ~1k items or when progress reporting matters,
use POST /item-banks/ingest-jobs instead. This endpoint blocks the
request thread and skips the cross-validation stage (which is worth its
latency only for the async flow).

Async ingest: stash the payload, enqueue an RQ job, return `job_id`.

Frontend polls GET /item-banks/ingest-jobs/{id} at ~1Hz. The worker
streams stage/percent/row back into the row's `progress` JSONB column
every ~100 items.

get.description: List ingest jobs I created (or all jobs if I'm an administrator).

schema.title: Response List Ingest Jobs Item Banks Ingest Jobs Get

get.description: Poll a single ingest job. Frontend hits this at ~1Hz while pending.

List active test configurations, including derived type and item count.

The 'type' field is computed from config.hybrid.enabled — UI uses it to
differentiate IRT-only from hybrid tests without re-parsing the JSONB.

schema.title: Response List Test Configs Test Configs  Get

post.description:
Create a new test config (researcher / administrator only).

Typical flow: clone an existing config, change min_sem / fusion_k /
neural_model_id / etc., save under a new name. The result becomes
available as a condition in /simulations/experiments.
Start an operational CAT session for the current user.

get.description:
Fetch a session. For in-progress sessions, populates next_item by
asking mirtcat which item it would serve right now — necessary for
resume-on-reconnect flows (a client that lost state needs to know the
pending item). Costs ~one R round-trip; only paid when needed.

Re-fetch the current next item for the session — useful for browser
refresh / resume scenarios. Calls mirtCAT to ask which item it would
select right now at the session's current theta state.

204.description: Session is complete; no further items.

post.description: Submit a response, run the hybrid fusion + selection cycle, persist.

get.description: List experiments. Researchers see their own; administrators see all.

schema.title: Response List Experiments Simulations Experiments Get

post.description: Enqueue a perturbation-validation experiment from a compact spec.

delete.description:
Hard-delete an experiment + its conditions + per-simulee results.

FK cascade configured at the DB layer (ON DELETE CASCADE on
simulation_conditions and simulation_results), so a single DELETE
on simulation_experiments triggers full cleanup.

Researchers can delete their own; administrators can delete any.
Refuses to delete experiments that are currently running — abort first.

get.description:
Aggregated metrics. Returns results_summary computed by worker on
completion. For experiments still running, returns whatever conditions
have been computed so far via partial aggregation.

post.description: Soft-abort. Sets status=aborted; worker checks this each iteration.

get.description:
List all neural models present in the registry. Sorted by model_id.

Reads metadata.json from each /models/model_N directory; skips
directories without a readable metadata.json (e.g. in-progress trainings).

schema.title: Response List Neural Models Neural Models  Get

get.description: List users with role/active filtering, substring search, and pagination.

post.description: Create a user with explicit role assignment.

patch.description: Change role, activation status, or password for a target user.

delete.description:
Deactivate a user (soft delete — preserves session history).

Idempotent. Returns 204 whether the user is already inactive or not.

get.description: Run liveness probes against every backing service in parallel.

post.description:
Run an end-to-end simulated session for the named user against each
test_config_id, in series. Returns full trajectories so the frontend
can plot theta + SE over N side-by-side.

Requires researcher or administrator role.

get.description:
List demo cohort users with their known true_theta values.

Used by the frontend Compare page to populate its dropdown without
hardcoding the cohort list. Researcher/admin only.

Per-examinee composite scores across all assessments in the course.

Builds one GradebookExamineeRow per examinee who has at least one
completed session in any of the course's assessments. Composite is
computed only over assessments with completed sessions, but the
`completion` field reports the fraction of all assessments completed.
Per-examinee view: same data as a gradebook row but for the caller only.

post.description:
Enqueue a cohort simulation job. Returns the job id for polling.

Researcher / administrator only.

get.description:
Poll a cohort-simulation job's status.

Status values map to RQ's job lifecycle: queued / started / finished /
failed / deferred / scheduled / canceled / stopped.

post.description:
Enqueue a response-replay job: re-score historical sessions on
source_test_config_id under each of the course's target assessments.

Returns the same shape as POST /simulate-cohort so the polling endpoint
can be reused.

get.description:
Readiness probe — pings postgres, redis, mirtcat-service and
neural-service in parallel (task #107). 200 only when all are healthy;
503 with per-dependency detail otherwise. Unauthenticated by design
(orchestrator probes); exposes no data beyond dependency health.

BlueprintBinding.title: BlueprintBinding
BlueprintBinding.description:
Attach a tests-platform blueprint to a CAT config (BP-MODES-1 §5).

The blueprint JSON is EMBEDDED verbatim at config-creation time (fetched
from tests-platform /api/v1/blueprints/{id} or pasted) — never referenced
live — so Ignite runs without a live tests-platform and configs are
self-describing for replay and simulation. Validated on save against the
vendored contract schema (app.contracts).

`on_nonconformant` is the scoring policy when a session completes with an
unsatisfiable minimum at max_items (spec §3.4(2)): 'flag' keeps the score
and marks the record; 'invalidate_score' additionally withholds the
scaled-score report.

CalibrationStatus.title: CalibrationStatus
CalibrationStatus.description:
How an item's IRT parameters were determined.

The boundary between item-factory / analysis-engine / CAT-platform is
this field: ingestion validates which sources are acceptable; the
CAT engine optionally refuses items whose status isn't in the test
config's allow_calibration_statuses list.

username.title: Username
username.description: Cohort or demo user to run sessions for

test_config_ids.title: Test Config Ids
test_config_ids.description: Two or more test configs to run side-by-side (typically [1, 2] for TRAD-CAT and Hybrid)

true_theta.title: True Theta
true_theta.description: True ability for 2PL response simulation. Falls back to known cohort value if username is in the registry.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: RNG seed for response sampling (controlled comparison)

CourseAssessmentPublic.title: CourseAssessmentPublic
CourseAssessmentPublic.description: Outbound view — includes the test config name for display.

CourseSummary.title: CourseSummary
CourseSummary.description: GET /courses/ list entry — lighter than CoursePublic.

CourseUpdate.title: CourseUpdate
CourseUpdate.description: Body for PATCH /courses/{id}. All fields optional.

FeatureGenerator.title: FeatureGenerator
FeatureGenerator.description: How to generate auxiliary features for hybrid experiments.

FusionType.title: FusionType
FusionType.description: Hybrid neural-IRT fusion weight function shape.

GradebookAssessmentRow.title: GradebookAssessmentRow
GradebookAssessmentRow.description: One assessment column in a per-examinee gradebook row.

GradebookExamineeRow.title: GradebookExamineeRow
GradebookExamineeRow.description: One examinee's grade row across the course's assessments.

IRTModelType.title: IRTModelType
IRTModelType.description:
IRT models supported by mirt/mirtCAT (full enumeration).

Reference: mirt package reference, generate.mirt_object() and mirt()
itemtype argument. Each value passes directly to mirtcat-service which
uses mirt's `generate.mirt_object(parameters, itemtype=...)`.

IngestJobPublic.title: IngestJobPublic
IngestJobPublic.description: Row shape returned by the async ingest-jobs endpoints.

IngestMode.title: IngestMode
IngestMode.description: How to handle (item_bank_id, item_index) collisions on ingest.

ItemBankIngestRequest.title: ItemBankIngestRequest
ItemBankIngestRequest.description:
POST /item-banks/ingest body.

Either creates a new bank (when item_bank_id is None) or upserts into
an existing one. Source format is one of:
  - 'json_inline': items list inline in this request body
  - 'csv_inline':  items as a CSV string in `csv_text`
Extended formats (task #95): 'xlsx_b64' (base64-encoded workbook),
'rds_b64' (base64-encoded R serialized bank; parsed via mirtcat-service).
QTI XML still deferred — wait for a concrete vendor.

ItemBankSummary.title: ItemBankSummary
ItemBankSummary.description: List entry for the item-banks dropdown.

ItemPublic.title: ItemPublic
ItemPublic.description:
Item content returned to the examinee — never includes correct_answer
or psychometric parameters.

MixtureComponent.title: MixtureComponent
MixtureComponent.description: One component of a mixture-of-normals population.

NeuralModelSummary.title: NeuralModelSummary
NeuralModelSummary.description: What the UI needs to render a "pick a model" dropdown.

PerturbationExperimentRequest.title: PerturbationExperimentRequest
PerturbationExperimentRequest.description:
Simplified spec for a perturbation-validation experiment.

The base_test_config_id must point at a hybrid config (hybrid.enabled=True).
Each condition sweeps population.target_feature_corr, holding everything
else constant — that isolates "does Hybrid's advantage hold up as the
neural prior weakens?" from other sources of variance.

PresentationConfig.title: PresentationConfig
PresentationConfig.description:
Examinee-facing UI settings that don't affect the psychometrics.

Kept as a separate block so it's clear these are display concerns,
not engine concerns. Values are consumed by the frontend only.

ProficiencyBand.title: ProficiencyBand
ProficiencyBand.description:
One band in a proficiency/letter-grade scale.

A band claims everything from theta_min upward UNTIL the next band's
theta_min. The lowest band typically has theta_min=None (= -infinity).

ResponseResult.title: ResponseResult
ResponseResult.description: Result of submitting a response — theta state + next item or completion.

ScoreReportPayload.title: ScoreReportPayload
ScoreReportPayload.description:
Examinee-facing scaled score derived from final_theta/final_se.

Populated only when the test config has score_reporting.enabled = true.
Raw theta/se remain in final_theta/final_se for researchers; this is the
block real examinees see.

ScoreReportingConfig.title: ScoreReportingConfig
ScoreReportingConfig.description:
How to transform theta into the examinee-facing scaled score.

Default: linear transform `score = scale_mean + scale_sd * theta`, clipped
to [scale_min, scale_max]. With defaults (mean=75, sd=10, range 0-100),
theta=0 -> 75 (C+ in the default band table), theta=+1.8 -> 93 (A+).

SelectionCriterion.title: SelectionCriterion
SelectionCriterion.description: All item selection criteria from mirtCAT 'criteria' parameter (page 21-22).

SessionCreate.title: SessionCreate
SessionCreate.description: Body for POST /sessions — examinee starts a test.

SessionPublic.title: SessionPublic
SessionPublic.description: Session state returned to the examinee/admin UI.

SessionResults.title: SessionResults
SessionResults.description: Full results bundle for the examinee results view + downloads.

SimulationCondition.title: SimulationCondition
SimulationCondition.description:
One experimental condition.

`population` is optional; when set, this condition draws its simulees from
the per-condition population instead of the experiment-level one. Used to
e.g. compare "Hybrid at N(0,1)" vs "Hybrid at mixture" within one
experiment. When None, the experiment-level `population` is used.

SimuleePopulation.title: SimuleePopulation
SimuleePopulation.description:
How to generate simulees.

distribution semantics:
  normal       theta ~ N(mean[0], sqrt(covariance[0][0])). Unidim.
  mvn          theta_vec ~ MVN(mean, covariance). Multidim.
  uniform      theta ~ U(mean[0] - sqrt(cov[0][0]), mean[0] + sqrt(cov[0][0])).
               The 'covariance' is reinterpreted as the half-range squared.
  mixture      Sample from a weighted mixture of normals (mixture_components).
  custom       Use explicit_thetas list directly. n_simulees must match list length.

StoppingConfig.title: StoppingConfig
StoppingConfig.description: Stopping criteria - any combination can be active simultaneously.

TestConfig.title: TestConfig
TestConfig.description: Complete test configuration. Stored as JSONB and passed to mirtCAT.

TestConfigCreate.title: TestConfigCreate
TestConfigCreate.description:
Body for POST /test-configs/.

Researchers usually clone an existing config and tweak a few fields
(stopping.min_sem, hybrid.fusion_k, hybrid.neural_model_id, etc.).
This endpoint takes the full TestConfig — the frontend handles the
clone-and-tweak UX.

TestConfigPublic.title: TestConfigPublic
TestConfigPublic.description: Test config listing entry. type is derived: 'hybrid' if config.hybrid.enabled else 'irt_only'.

TrajectoryPoint.title: TrajectoryPoint
TrajectoryPoint.description:
One row in the session results trajectory.

session_started event: item_id/response/latency_ms are None.
response_recorded event: theta_* are values AFTER the response.

UserCreate.title: UserCreate
UserCreate.description:
Body for POST /admin/users.

Email validation is intentionally minimal — we accept any non-empty string
containing '@'. The platform supports demo / internal user accounts using
*.local TLDs that pydantic's strict EmailStr rejects, and operational
deliverability is the user's concern, not the schema's.

UserPublic.title: UserPublic
UserPublic.description: Minimal user shape returned by /auth/login and /auth/me.

UserSelfRegister.title: UserSelfRegister
UserSelfRegister.description:
Public /auth/register payload. Role is fixed to 'examinee' server-side;
administrator/researcher accounts must be created by an authenticated admin.
extra='forbid' so a client sneaking in role= gets a clear 422 instead of a
silently-demoted examinee account.
